storyInfo: Titi refuses to deceive Minos and decides to steal the fake harp. Kassandra, Lena and Hekádemos try to rescue their friend/patient/rival from prison.

attributes.content: Hey, you're the one who invited me. Say something. I skipped the banquet celebrating the completion of the First Temple for you, you know.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: You were a big part of getting the work across the finish line the day before the exchange ceremony. Tradition calls for a full day of celebration. The craftsmen began drinking and reciting poetry in broad daylight.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Not all of them hold their drink well. Guess the peace officers will have their hands full tonight.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Kantia from the moving crew, right? She's really taken to you after you helped her a few times.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yeah... it's been like a dream, coming to Athenius.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Right you are.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: First day in Athenius, you solved the dispute between the fruit farmers and craftsmen. Then you became the supervisor of the final phase of the temple rebuilding project.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: And you chased Xanthos over multiple blocks after the harp was stolen.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Way more interesting than just escorting a relic, isn't it?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yeah.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: But my job was to ensure that the exchange ceremony goes smoothly, and promote the friendship between our two nations.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: What's the matter, Titi? This isn't like you.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Pallas...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The harp of Kereseira we brought... it's a replica.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I would never have known, if the harp wasn't damaged during my scuffle with Xanthos.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Now, my deputy, Behnui, wants to fulfill the padishah's mission by handing over the fake harp in the exchange ceremony... but I know that's not right.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The ceremony is tomorrow. Do you have a solution?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: There's no good way to do this. We'd need a miracle.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I read through every relevant book I could borrow from the Bibliotheka of Athenius. I learned that the harp of Kereseira was lost to war a long time ago, and it hasn't been seen since.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: It's likely that the Sargonian craftsmen who made this replica had only seen the blueprints, not the harp itself.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Could the true harp still be in Minos? Or...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Or it was destroyed by the fires of war long ago... I'm sorry.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: There's no need for you to apologize.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: What do you plan to do, if the true harp is nowhere to be found?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I can't publicly announce in my capacity as Sargonian ambassador that we brought a fake harp. It's more likely to lead to conflict than to reveal the truth.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: A very reasonable concern.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: And so, I'll have to stop the ceremony in my personal capacity.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I'm getting rid of the fake harp before the ceremony can start.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You can't go in.
attributes.name: Jailer

attributes.content: Look, I know a special visitor's permit is required to visit and deliver supplies. I just forgot.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Can you accommodate me this once? My old classmate went in a couple of days ago, all of a sudden. His family asked me to bring him some stuff.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Alright, to tell you the truth, he's pretty sick.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: The Athenian jail has a doctor and basic medicine, but it's not as effective as the Rhodes Island stuff. He could have an attack while in custody, if he doesn't get proper treatment.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Save your breath, it's no use.
attributes.name: Jailer

attributes.content: Alright. I didn't want to do this...
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Here's my pass. Hekádemos, Grammateús to the Astynomos.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I am authorized to enter detention facilities, even bring in suspects for questioning. Open the door. This is an order. I will file the necessary paperwork later.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You want to see the two thieves who came in a few days ago?
attributes.name: Jailer
Hekádemos
attributes.content: Lykeion is not a...

attributes.name: Hekádemos
attributes.content: Never mind. Yes.
You can't.
attributes.name: Jailer

attributes.content: What? Why not?!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You are authorized to see prisoners held at the jail, but the two you're looking for aren't here anymore.
attributes.name: Jailer
Hekádemos
attributes.content: Were they transferred?

attributes.name: Hekádemos
attributes.content: That is unusual. Has Kyría Athopoulou's Oripathy treatment system been implemented already?
Where were they transferred?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Don't bother, Hekádemos. I don't know who they are to you, but not even the Grammateús to the Astynomos is authorized to see inmates on death row.
attributes.name: Jailer

attributes.content: Death row?!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Sorry, Kyría Athopoulou...
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: Does Lydia... I mean, does the Astynomos really not have the time?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: I heard that Xanthos and his apprentice were arrested for theft.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: They're my patients, and in a key stage of treatment, as the Rhodes Island clinic already reported.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Xanthos's Blood Originium-Crystal Density is particularly high, which could potentially lead to an acute attack of Oripathy. I would like to deliver medicine to them...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Look, I don't understand any of the medical jargon you're throwing around.
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: We are all very grateful to you for returning to Athenius, bringing back medical knowledge that you learned abroad and establishing an Oripathy clinic, but...
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: I shouldn't talk too much, but you're a good doctor, and the Astynomos' sister. You really should keep your distance from her for a few days at least, if only for the sake of your relationship.
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: Why?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: The official line says the crime was theft, but the Sargonian ambassador came here a few days ago and said the thing that was stolen wasn't the ambassador's personal item, but an important relic for the exchange ceremony.
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: He put a lot of pressure on Lydia. They're probably going to be publicly charged with treason, once the ceremony is over.
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: Treason?! But according to Minoan law—
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Most of the issues the Astynomos faces cannot be solved through legal procedures.
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: She wants a new, modern Minos, and she is willing to give everything for it. That's why we're willing to work for her.
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: Perhaps you're not aware, but she gave up her family inheritance, even chasing her other family out of Athenius after taking office...
attributes.name: Clerk
Lena
attributes.content: She gave up her inheritance?

attributes.name: Lena
attributes.content: I thought the business failed...

This exchange of relics is an important part of her administration. There must be no mistake, at least not on the surface... even if it means sacrificing two lives. It's not like they're innocent, after all.
attributes.name: Clerk

attributes.content: I'm sorry... I have to get back to work.
attributes.name: Clerk
Lena
attributes.content: What is Lydia...?

attributes.name: Lena
attributes.content: Well, she wouldn't meet with me, and I doubt I can convince her, even if we did meet.
But Xanthos and Lykeion need to be tried fairly. I can't let Lydia make a mistake like this. What do I do?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Sorry, I was lost in thought—
Kyría Athopoulou?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You've been staring out the window, Astynomos.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Apologies, Hiéreia Kassandra.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Why not let Kyría Athopoulou in? I have to discuss the establishment of an Oripathy treatment system with her anyway.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Oh? I thought you wanted to get extra help to plead the case of the two Infected who were arrested a few days ago.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You know that ferryman, Xanthos, quite well. I believe you were the one who found him the job at the crossing?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: The peace officers will deal with this theft. Neither of us need to worry about it.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I'm afraid I don't have that luxury. If this incident affects the trust between us and Sargon, then this incident will become more than a simple theft...
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: It's my own business. It has nothing to do with a priestess of Endymion Temple.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Back on topic, Hiéreia Kassandra. The opening play for tomorrow's exchange ceremony has been decided. It is to be King Pertheus, a popular classic across Terra.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: All the priests need to do is attend. There will be no need for complicated rituals.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: A few weeks ago, I came to discuss the priest-directed ceremonial play for the completion of Kereseira's First Temple. I have not seen you at the Astynomos' Office since.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I apologize. My schedule is a busy one. I certainly remember. The exchange ceremony also requires an opening play, and we can kill two fowlbeasts with one stone this way. We need not bother the priests, however.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Excuse me, but the rebuilding of Kereseira's First Temple is a symbol of the people's spirit. Now that it is complete, tradition calls for a proper ceremony to invite the heroes to enter the temple and give Minos their blessings.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The council is not trying to usurp your administrative power, and the exchange ceremony does fall within your responsibility.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: However, Minoan drama began as a ritualistic performance.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Every single detail is carefully arranged, from the props to the program to the performers. It's not enough to simply put on a popular play.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: 'Immortal legend'... 'witness of the oracle'... 'heroic relic'...
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Do not think that I am completely ignorant of traditional ceremony, or that I'm one of those radicals who calls for every tradition to be abolished.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: But even if you have your choice of the program and control over the troupe, there is still something missing from the traditional ritual that you want, isn't there?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: ...The Sargonian mission brought Kereseira's harp.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Oh? You do recognize the relic's ritual significance?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: ...What do you mean?
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I'm neither an archaeologist nor a historian. As Astynomos, however, I understand every priest who sits on the council, including you.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You are the head priestess of Endymion Temple, and endowed with the divine gift of speaking on behalf of the heroes. Your knowledge of heroic epics puts most scholars to shame.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Yet you showed no interest about the harp of Kereseira that the Sargonian mission brought with them. You didn't even ask to see it.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I don't know why exactly, but I can tell that the harp the Sargonian mission brought was not the heroic relic that you knew.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: The ceremonial play could not have been performed to begin with.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: No, I already...
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I have nothing against the council, Hiéreia Kassandra. If you present a legitimate heroic relic for the ceremony, I would be more than happy to give the choice of play back to you.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: For the past century, however, the temples have mostly used crude replicas in place of true relics for your ceremonies. I don't need to tell you that the link between us and the heroes is weakening.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: As a modern state, Minos can no longer rely on ancient legends to unite our people's spirit. No need to worry, however. The temples and priests will continue to exist.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You can continue to contribute to historical research and tourism.
attributes.name: Lydia
